title: Avoid duplicate parallel builds with same Dockerfile
body:
### Feature request description

Create the following docker-compose manifest:

```yaml
services:
  abc:
    build: .
  xyz:
    build: .
```

And then run `podman compose` (which uses docker-compose talking to a Podman socket). The same image is built twice in parallel.

This is different from the Docker daemon behavior: https://github.com/docker/compose/issues/7343#issuecomment-863008575

### Suggest potential solution

When starting to build an image, check if an identical build is already in progress. If so, wait for that existing build to complete.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body:
@emersion Thanks for creating the issue, Not sure but i think in this case client is making two requests to podman server and it is performing build for both of them, podman parallel build should be safe and layers should not be built again if they were built before but some parts can be built twice because of the race, I think unlike docker there is no daemon in between which performs deduplication of build requests.

Could you please specify what issues are you facing because of this behavior ?

There is a daemon though, since there is a Podman socket.

The issue is that some images are built many times in duplicate. Right now on my project the same image is built 4 times at the same time and this number will increase as we add more containers.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
